---
title: "LRP-CA-003: Concurrent conditional associations — skills → taught receptive vocabulary (per wave)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Fable 5); substantially edited by a LLM-based AI tool (Codex/GPT-5).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP-CA-003 extends the **concurrent conditional-associations** family (#312,
descriptive-association workstream #314) to **taught receptive vocabulary** (block 1,
`TR`) as the focal outcome. At each timepoint (t1–t4) it fits a between-child
Beta-Binomial regression of taught receptive vocabulary's level on the standardised
same-wave logits of the other core skills — word reading (`W`), letter sounds (`L`),
blending (`B`), taught expressive vocabulary (`TE`), and receptive (`R`) and expressive
(`E`) vocabulary — plus age and a group nuisance term. The four waves are fitted
separately and reported side by side.

The family's core skill set is {`W`, `L`, `B`, `TR`, `TE`, `R`, `E`}: each model
conditions its focal outcome on the remaining six, so alongside `lrp-rli-ca-001`
(word reading) and `lrp-rli-ca-002` (letter sounds) the models describe the
conditional joint distribution of the same measure set from different sides. The
taught expressive companion `TE` stays in the predictor set — the two taught tests are strongly correlated, but the regularising priors stabilise the fit. The adjusted-versus-bivariate contrast shows sensitivity to the conditioning set; it is not a decomposition of shared variance. Note `TR` approaches its 24-item
ceiling at later waves: the Beta-Binomial respects the bound, but the outcome's
variance (and so the associations' resolution) compresses as scores stack near the
top. All children are on the intervention from t2, so these describe the conditional
joint distribution of the skill measures within a treated system.

::: {.callout-note title="Estimand: descriptive association (no causal warrant)"}
Every coefficient is an **adjusted association**. Conditioning on contemporaneous (post-treatment) skill levels is intentional and licensed *because nothing here is read causally*. Read the results with the Table-2-fallacy, measurement-error and collinearity/regularisation caveats set out in the Results section.
:::

## Design

- **Four separate cross-sectional fits** (one row per child per wave), reported side by side — not one stacked model with a child random intercept, which would tilt the coefficients toward a within-child quantity and change the estimand (issue #312 decision). The wave with the largest complete-outcome sample (ties → latest) is the operational diagnostic anchor for the standard trace and plots; this does not make it substantively primary or establish that it is best-powered. Every adjusted and bivariate fit has complete convergence metrics in the tables.
- **Predictors** enter as standardised same-wave logits with regularising
  `Normal(0, 0.3)` slopes (essential at n ≈ 53 with a collinear predictor cluster).
  Floored measures (`P`, `N`) are excluded as predictors.
- **Group** is included only as a non-interpretable nuisance term (absorbs arm
  composition at each wave); it is not reported as an association.
- **Missing predictor values** are mean-imputed (0 on the standardised scale). This can bias a conditional coefficient, with no guaranteed direction when missingness relates to the predictor, outcome or other skills; observed and imputed counts are reported. Rows missing the focal *outcome* are dropped.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_concurrent.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
